summary: Mutation testing infrastructure failed: Stryker vitest runner could not find test files related to mutated files. This is a known incompatibility between Stryker's vitest.related mode and .js extension imports in ESM codebases. No mutants were tested.
testQuality: Manual review of tests shows good behavioral coverage: server name/version, capabilities shape, exactly 3 tools registered in correct order, each tool's schema properties, callback presence, and transport connection. The types.ts file (toolSuccess/toolError) lacks dedicated unit tests but is indirectly exercised through tool handler tests. No surviving mutants to report due to infrastructure failure.

suggestions.description: toolSuccess and toolError have no dedicated unit tests — only indirectly covered via tool handler mocks
suggestions.rationale: Mutations to toolSuccess (e.g. changing content array structure, isError flag) would survive since create-server tests mock the handlers and never exercise types.ts directly
suggestions.suggestion: Add unit tests: expect(toolSuccess({ key: 'val' })).toEqual({ content: [{ type: 'text', text: '{"key":"val"}' }] }) and expect(toolError(new Error('boom'))).toEqual({ isError: true, content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'boom' }] })

suggestions.description: toolError branch for non-Error values (String(err)) is not tested
suggestions.rationale: The ternary err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) has an untested branch for non-Error values like strings or objects
suggestions.suggestion: Add test: expect(toolError('plain string error')).toEqual({ isError: true, content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'plain string error' }] })
